events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. We are assessing transmitter installation. Condition: DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service (gas taps below pipe / liquid taps on top - the classic). Work rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. This is non-compliant. Risk? For DP impulse lines wrong slope can cause measurement error, liquid accumulation in gas lines, gas pockets in liquid lines, potentially inaccurate control, but not immediately dangerous? Could be high if safety instrumented function. But generic manufacturing floor, maybe medium/high. Need choose. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70. Value not asked 0. Sound etc default. Findings: impulse lines slope incorrect; gas service taps below pipe causing condensate accumulation; liquid service taps on top causing gas pocketing; not per API RP 551. Actions: re-route/rework impulse tubing to correct slope, orient taps per service (gas on top/side, liquid on side/bottom), verify pitch toward drain/condensate pot or seal pot as applicable, re-inspect before closeout. Rationale. References: API RP 551, manufacturer manuals. Need valid JSON only, no prose. Ensure fields present.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "DP transmitter impulse lines are sloped incorrectly for the intended service.",
    "Gas service impulse taps are located below the process pipe, allowing condensate to collect in the lines.",
    "Liquid service impulse taps are located on top of the process pipe, allowing gas pockets to form.",
    "Incorrect orientation will cause erroneous differential pressure measurement and unreliable transmitter output.",
    "Installation does not conform to API RP 551 or typical manufacturer guidance."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Rework and re-route impulse lines to the correct slope and tap orientation for the service.",
    "For gas service, locate taps on the top or upper side of the pipe and slope lines toward a condensate pot or drain.",
    "For liquid service, locate taps on the side or lower portion of the pipe and slope lines to avoid gas pockets.",
    "Re-inspect and verify transmitter zero/span and impulse line integrity before closeout."
  ],
  "rationale": "The impulse tubing is installed backwards for the service conditions: gas taps below the pipe will trap liquid and liquid taps on top will trap gas, producing inaccurate DP readings. This is a non-compliant, defective rough-in that must be reworked.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551 - Process Control Instrumentation",
    "Manufacturer transmitter installation manuals"
  ]
}
